attributes.content:
## Overview

The Recipe card gives the workspace a structured home for cooking recipe: ingredients, steps, prep/cook time, servings, nutrition, and photo.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Recipe acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Calendar]]** -- Calendar can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Recipe.
- **[[Task]]** -- Recipe often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Recipe.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Recipe can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Recipe can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Timer]]** -- Timer can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Recipe.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Recipe as the structured home for cooking recipe: ingredients, steps, prep/cook time, servings, nutrition, and photo and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Recipe when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Recipe stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
